children.name: Anous
children.description: Noddies — dark-plumaged tropical terns with distinctive pale or white caps, breeding on tropical islands across all three major oceans. Unlike most terns, noddies do not plunge-dive but snatch prey from the surface, and they often rest on the sea rather than staying constantly airborne.

children.name: Anous albivitta
children.description: The grey noddy or grey ternlet (Anous albivitta) is a seabird belonging to the family Laridae..

children.name: Anous ceruleus
children.description: The blue noddy or hinaokū or manuohina (Anous ceruleus) is a seabird in the family Laridae..

children.name: Anous niger
children.description: Anous niger - a species in the genus Anous, family Laridae.

children.name: Chlidonias
children.description: Marsh terns — dark-bodied freshwater terns that pick insects and small prey from the surface of marshes rather than plunge-diving.

children.name: Chroicocephalus
children.description: Hooded and black-headed gulls — smaller than Larus, characterised in summer by dark hoods that range from black to chocolate brown. The black-headed gull (skrattmås) is the most numerous gull in Stockholm and across Sweden's inland waters: its breeding colonies produce a cacophony of laughing calls that give it its Swedish name ('skratt' = laugh). Despite the name, the summer hood is actually dark chocolate brown, not black. Black-headed gulls are inland birds as much as coastal ones, following ploughs, roosting on city parks, and commuting between feeding areas and inland reservoirs in spectacular evening flights.

children.name: Chroicocephalus cucullatus
children.description: Chroicocephalus cucullatus - a species in the genus Chroicocephalus, family Laridae.

children.name: Chroicocephalus hartlaubii
children.description: Hartlaub's gull (Chroicocephalus hartlaubii) is a small gull in the genus Chroicocephalus..

children.name: Chroicocephalus minutus
children.description: The world's smallest gull at just 28 cm. Black underwing and black hood in summer. Breeds in Scandinavian and Baltic marshes. Occurs regularly on passage along Swedish coasts. Has a delicate, tern-like flight, dipping to the surface to pick off insects.

children.name: Chroicocephalus saundersi
children.description: Saunders's gull (Saundersilarus saundersi) or Chinese black-headed gull, is a species of gull in the family Laridae..

children.name: Larus franklinii
children.description: Larus franklinii - a species in the genus Chroicocephalus, family Laridae.

children.name: Creagrus
children.description: Creagrus - a genus in the family Laridae.

children.name: Creagrus furcatus
children.description: The swallow-tailed gull (Creagrus furcatus) is an equatorial seabird in the gull family, Laridae..

children.name: Feducciavis
children.description: Feducciavis - a genus in the family Laridae.

children.name: Feducciavis loftini
children.description: Feducciavis loftini - a species in the genus Feducciavis, family Laridae.

children.name: Gaviota
children.description: Gaviota - a genus in the family Laridae.

children.name: Gaviota niobrara
children.description: Gaviota niobrara is an extinct species of gull that lived during the Miocene..

children.name: Gelochelidon
children.description: Gull-billed terns — stout, short-billed terns that hawk insects and small vertebrates over land as well as water.

children.name: Gelochelidon macrotarsa
children.description: The Australian tern or Australian gull-billed tern (Gelochelidon macrotarsa) is a tern in the family Laridae, native to Australia..

children.name: Gygis
children.description: White terns — ethereally beautiful, entirely snow-white tropical seabirds that famously lay their single egg on a bare branch with no nest. The large blue eye and translucent wings give them an otherworldly appearance.

children.name: Hydrocoloeus
children.description: Hydrocoloeus - a genus in the family Laridae.

children.name: Hydrocoloeus minutus
children.description: The little gull (Hydrocoloeus minutus) is a species of gull belonging to the family Laridae which is mainly found in the Palearctic with some small colonies in North America..

children.name: Hydroprogne
children.description: A monotypic genus for the Caspian tern, the largest tern in the world.

children.name: Ichthyaetus
children.description: Large dark-hooded gulls of southern Eurasia and Africa, several with striking black or grey hoods in breeding plumage.

children.name: Ichthyaetus melanocephalus x Chroicocephalus ridibundus
children.description: Ichthyaetus melanocephalus x Chroicocephalus ridibundus - a species in the genus Ichthyaetus, family Laridae.

children.name: Ichthyaetus melanocephalus x Larus canus
children.description: Ichthyaetus melanocephalus x Larus canus - a species in the genus Ichthyaetus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larosterna
children.description: A monotypic genus containing only the Inca tern, one of the most striking and unusual seabirds in the world, unique to the cold Humboldt Current system of the South American Pacific coast.

children.name: Larus
children.description: Large gulls — the dominant seabirds of northern European coastlines and the most familiar waterbirds in Swedish cities. Gulls are supremely opportunistic generalists: they eat fish, invertebrates, carrion, garbage, and other birds' eggs and chicks with equal facility. Their intelligence is remarkable — herring gulls have been documented dropping shellfish onto hard surfaces to break them open, and individuals can recognise specific human faces. Stockholm's rocky archipelago supports large colonies of herring gulls and lesser black-backed gulls, whose calls are the ambient soundtrack of the Swedish coast. 'Gull identification' has become one of birding's most obsessive niches, with hybrids, unusual ages, and rare visitors drawing dedicated specialists.

children.name: Laroides chalcopterus
children.description: Laroides chalcopterus - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus arcticus
children.description: Larus arcticus - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus argentatus x hyperboreus
children.description: Larus argentatus x hyperboreus - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus argentatus x marinus
children.description: Larus argentatus x marinus - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus argenteus
children.description: Larus argenteus - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus atlanticus
children.description: Category:Southeastern South American coastal fauna Category:Near threatened animals Category:Near threatened biota of South America.

children.name: Larus bulleri
children.description: The black-billed gull (Chroicocephalus bulleri), also called Buller's gull or tarāpuka (Māori), is a Near Threatened species of gull in the family Laridae..

children.name: Larus cachinnans x argentatus
children.description: Larus cachinnans x argentatus - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus cachinnans x michahellis
children.description: Larus cachinnans x michahellis - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus cinerarius
children.description: Larus cinerarius - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus dolnicensis
children.description: Larus dolnicensis - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus elmorei
children.description: Larus elmorei is an extinct species of gull that lived during the mid-Pliocene..

children.name: Larus flavipes
children.description: Larus flavipes - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus fuscus
children.description: The lesser black-backed gull is a large gull that breeds on the Atlantic and Arctic coasts of Europe. It is migratory, wintering from the British Isles south to West Africa. However, it has increased dramatically in North America, especially along the east coast.

children.name: Larus fuscus x argentatus
children.description: Larus fuscus x argentatus - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus glaucodes
children.description: Larus glaucodes - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus hutchinsii
children.description: Larus hutchinsii - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus icthyaetus
children.description: Larus icthyaetus - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus kamtschatschensis
children.description: Larus kamtschatschensis - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus lacus
children.description: Larus lacus is an extinct species of gull that lived in North America during the Late Pliocene..

children.name: Larus leucopterus
children.description: Larus leucopterus - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus medius
children.description: Larus medius - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus michahellis
children.description: The yellow-legged gull or western yellow-legged gull is a large gull found in Europe, the Middle East and North Africa, which has only recently achieved wide recognition as a distinct species. It was formerly treated as a subspecies of either the Caspian gull L. cachinnans, or more broadly as a subspecies of the herring gull L.

children.name: Larus naumanni
children.description: A large, pale-mantled gull of Mongolian and Chinese plateau lakes, closely allied to the yellow-legged gull complex. Breeds on high-altitude inland lakes across Mongolia and adjacent China, and winters along the coasts of eastern China and Korea. Only recently widely accepted as a full species separate from the Caspian gull, following genetic analysis.

children.name: Larus niveus
children.description: Larus niveus - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus oregonus
children.description: Larus oregonus is an extinct species of gull that lived during the Late Pleistocene..

children.name: Larus perpetuus
children.description: Larus perpetuus is an extinct species of gull that lived in North America during the Pliocene..

children.name: Larus pristinus
children.description: Larus pristinus - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus raemdonckii
children.description: Larus raemdonckii - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus robustus
children.description: Larus robustus is an extinct species of gull that lived during the Late Pleistocene..

children.name: Larus septentrionalis
children.description: Larus septentrionalis - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus spec
children.description: Larus spec - a species in the genus Larus, family Laridae.

children.name: Larus utunui
children.description: The Huahine gull (Chroicocephalus utunui), also known as the Society Islands gull, is an extinct bird, a species of gull of which subfossil bones were found at the Fa'ahia archeological site on Huahine, in the Society Islands of French Polynesia..

children.name: Leucophaeus
children.description: American hooded gulls including the abundant laughing gull and the desert-nesting grey gull.

children.name: Onychoprion
children.description: Tropical seabird terns with dark upperparts that spend years continuously at sea before returning to breed on remote islands.

children.name: Onychoprion aleuticus
children.description: The Aleutian tern is a migratory bird living in the subarctic region of the globe most of the year. It is frequently associated with the Arctic tern, which it closely resembles. While both species have a black cap, the Aleutian tern may be distinguished by its white forehead.

children.name: Pagophila
children.description: A monotypic genus for the ivory gull, the only entirely white gull, tied to the high Arctic pack ice.

children.name: Larus brachytarsus
children.description: Larus brachytarsus - a species in the genus Pagophila, family Laridae.

children.name: Pagophila eburnea
children.description: #REDIRECT Ivory gull Category:Taxa named by Constantine Phipps, 2nd Baron Mulgrave.

children.name: Phaetusa
children.description: Phaetusa - a genus in the family Laridae.

children.name: Phaetusa simplex
children.description: The large-billed tern (Phaetusa simplex) is a species of tern in the family Laridae..

children.name: Procelsterna
children.description: Blue noddies — tiny, pale terns of the tropical Pacific, the most delicate members of the Laridae, feeding on zooplankton and tiny fish by dipping to the sea surface.

children.name: Pseudosterna
children.description: Pseudosterna - a genus in the family Laridae.

children.name: Pseudosterna degener
children.description: Pseudosterna degener - a species in the genus Pseudosterna, family Laridae.

children.name: Pseudosterna papeana
children.description: Pseudosterna papeana - a species in the genus Pseudosterna, family Laridae.

children.name: Rhodostethia
children.description: Rhodostethia - a genus in the family Laridae.

children.name: Rhodostethia rosea
children.description: Ross's gull (Rhodostethia rosea) is a small gull, the only species in its genus, although it has been suggested the genus should be merged with the closely related Hydrocoloeus, which otherwise only includes the little gull..

children.name: Rissa
children.description: Kittiwakes — small, gentle, truly pelagic gulls that nest on sheer sea cliffs and spend the rest of the year far out at sea.

children.name: Rissa brunnichii
children.description: Rissa brunnichii - a species in the genus Rissa, family Laridae.

children.name: Rissa estesi
children.description: Rissa estesi is an extinct species of bird in the genus Rissa that lived during the Piacenzian stage of the Neogene period..

children.name: Rissa kotzebui
children.description: Rissa kotzebui - a species in the genus Rissa, family Laridae.

children.name: Rissa tridactyla
children.description: The black-legged kittiwake is a seabird species in the gull family Laridae. This species was first described by Carl Linnaeus in his landmark 1758 10th edition of Systema Naturae as Larus tridactylus. The English name is derived from its call, a shrill 'kittee-wa-aaake, kitte-wa-aaake'.

children.name: Rynchops
children.description: Skimmers — unique terns with a lower mandible longer than the upper, used to slice through the water surface in flight to snap up fish.

children.name: Rynchops flavirostris
children.description: The African skimmer is a species of bird belonging to the skimmer genus Rynchops in the family Laridae. It is found along rivers, lakes and lagoons in Sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Rynchops fulva
children.description: Rynchops fulva - a species in the genus Rynchops, family Laridae.

children.name: Sterna
children.description: Sea terns — elegant, sharply pointed seabirds built for plunge-diving into the sea for small fish. Terns are the migrants par excellence: the Arctic tern (silvertärna) makes the longest migration of any animal on Earth, breeding in the Arctic (including northern Sweden's coast) and wintering in the Antarctic, covering 70,000–90,000 km annually — it experiences more daylight than any other creature. Common terns (fisktärna) breed in noisy, fiercely defensive colonies on Swedish coastal rocks and lake islands. Terns dive at speed into the water to catch fish, folding their wings at the last moment. Their aggressive colony defence — dive-bombing and occasionally striking intruders — makes tern colonies an exciting experience for visiting naturalists.

children.name: Sterna australis
children.description: Sterna australis - a species in the genus Sterna, family Laridae.

children.name: Sterna fuliginosa
children.description: Sterna fuliginosa - a species in the genus Sterna, family Laridae.

children.name: Sterna galericulata
children.description: Sterna galericulata - a species in the genus Sterna, family Laridae.

children.name: Sterna hirundinacea
children.description: The South American tern (Sterna hirundinacea) is a species of tern found in coastal regions of southern South America, including the Falkland Islands, ranging north to Peru (Pacific coast) and Brazil (Atlantic coast)..

children.name: Sterna hirundo
children.description: The common tern is a seabird in the family Laridae. This bird has a circumpolar distribution, its four subspecies breeding in temperate and subarctic regions of Europe, Asia and North America. It is strongly migratory, wintering in coastal tropical and subtropical regions.

children.name: Sterna magna
children.description: Sterna magna - a species in the genus Sterna, family Laridae.

children.name: Sterna melanops
children.description: Sterna melanops - a species in the genus Sterna, family Laridae.

children.name: Sterna milneedwardsi
children.description: Sterna milneedwardsi - a species in the genus Sterna, family Laridae.

children.name: Sterna minuta
children.description: Sterna minuta - a species in the genus Sterna, family Laridae.

children.name: Sterna spec
children.description: Sterna spec - a species in the genus Sterna, family Laridae.

children.name: Sterna trudeaui
children.description: The snowy-crowned tern (Sterna trudeaui), also known as Trudeau's tern, is a species of bird in subfamily Sterninae of the family Laridae, the gulls, terns, and skimmers..

children.name: Sterna virgata
children.description: The Kerguelen tern (Sterna virgata) is a tern of the Southern Hemisphere..

children.name: Sternula
children.description: The smallest terns — fast-flying, yellow-billed species that hover and plunge into shallow water for tiny fish.

children.name: Sternula sinensis
children.description: A critically endangered small tern breeding on a tiny number of sandy beaches in China, Taiwan, and the Philippines. The global population is estimated at just a few thousand birds and has been severely impacted by coastal development. Conservation efforts in Taiwan using artificial nesting platforms have produced encouraging results.

children.name: Thalasseus
children.description: Crested terns — large terns with shaggy black crests and slender bills, plunge-diving for fish in warm and temperate seas.

children.name: Thalasseus albididorsalis
children.description: The West African crested tern (Thalasseus albididorsalis) is a bird species in the family Laridae..

children.name: Thalasseus bernsteini
children.description: The Chinese crested tern (Thalasseus bernsteini) is a tern in the family Laridae..

children.name: Thalasseus sandvicensis
children.description: The Sandwich tern is a tern in the family Laridae. It is very closely related to the lesser crested tern, Chinese crested tern, Cabot's tern, and elegant tern and has been known to interbreed with both elegant and lesser crested terns. It breeds in the Palearctic from Europe to the Caspian Sea, and winters in the Mediterranean and on the coasts of Africa, India, and Sri Lanka.

children.name: Thalasseus zimmermanni
children.description: One of the world's rarest birds — fewer than 100 individuals are known to exist — breeding only on a handful of rocky islands off China and Taiwan. Distinguished from the greater crested tern by its yellow bill with a black tip and white-based black crest. Rediscovered breeding in 2000 after 63 years without confirmed records; listed as Critically Endangered.

children.name: Xema
children.description: A monotypic genus for Sabine's gull, a small, tern-like Arctic gull with a boldly patterned wing.
